title: AMC Hits $1 Billion Revenue as Blockbusters Revive Box Office
text:
AMC Theatres achieved impressive first-quarter 2026 results, reporting revenue of $1.04 billion, which exceeded analyst projections of $997 million. The theater chain attributed this success to robust ticket sales both domestically and internationally, driven by major releases including Avatar Fire and Ash, Scream 7, Project Hail Mary, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, and Michael.

The company significantly improved its financial position compared to the prior year. The quarterly loss narrowed to $117 million, or 36 cents per share, compared to a $202 million loss in the same period last year. CEO Adam Aron highlighted that the company posted its best adjusted EBITDA result since 2019, before the pandemic.

DEBT MANAGEMENT AND FINANCIAL RESTRUCTURING
AMC addressed its substantial debt burden through strategic financial maneuvers. The theater chain refinanced $400 million of debt originally due in 2027, extending the maturity to 2031 while reducing annual cash interest expenses. Additionally, the company converted $155 million of debt into equity and raised approximately $72 million through its at-the-market equity program.

The chain also benefited from selling part of its Hycroft mining company investment, generating $54 million in cash from an initial $27.9 million investment.

FUTURE OUTLOOK
Management expects the second quarter to be profitable, supported by upcoming blockbusters and sustained international market growth. The studio commitment to maintaining a minimum 45-day theatrical window provided additional confidence in the company's recovery trajectory.
